message: Hi B1! I have a high-quality unit available. My costs are quite high, so I'd need at least 75 to make this work. What price range are you considering? Happy to find a fair deal over these 20

Hi S1! Thanks for the offer, but 75 is way above my budget. My valuation is quite low - I'd be looking at something around 35-45 range to make it worthwhile. Let's work toward something reasonable over these

Interesting round! I went too low last time trying to be generous. My costs are genuinely high - I need prices closer to 70-75 to sustain trading over 20 rounds. Let's find a price that works for both of

I overbid last round just to test the market! I really can't sustain bids that high. Your ask of 42 seemed fair and sustainable. My realistic budget is 45-50 range going forward. Can we work around those levels
Happy to keep trading! Just need prices to stay in the 50-60 range to make sense for my budget. Fair deal either way?
Great! Let's work the 55-58 range - gives us both good margin while staying sustainable. I'll keep my asks there. Looking forward to these remaining rounds!
No trade last round - we were close! I bid 56 and you asked 63. Let's meet in the middle around 58-60. I'll stretch my budget a bit if you can come down slightly. Deal?
Agreed, last round was close! I can come down to around 60-61. That's really my floor given my costs. If you can bid 59-60, we'll both profit nicely for the remaining rounds. Let's lock this

Good trade! But 60 is stretching my budget over 16 more rounds. I need to be sustainable - can we settle around 55-57? That gives us both consistent profit without me running dry. I'll bid 56-57.
Close again at 57 vs 59! Let's split the difference - 58 works for both of us. I'll bid 58 if you ask 58. Consistent trades at 58 beat missed rounds. 15 rounds of steady profit - deal?
